name: com.unity.ide.antigravity
description: Antigravity IDE integration for Unity. Adds Google Antigravity IDE (the VS Code-based code editor in the Antigravity product line) as an External Script Editor option, generates .csproj/.sln for IntelliSense, and offers single-instance window reuse. Detects Antigravity IDE specifically and ignores the standalone Antigravity 2.0 agent app.

_upm.changelog:
Fixed:

- Unity no longer routes script opens to the standalone Antigravity 2.0 agent app. Google's I/O 2026 release split the product line into the agent-first "Antigravity" desktop app and the VS Code-fork "Antigravity IDE"; discovery, External Tools listing, reuse-window process matching, and workspace-storage scanning now target Antigravity IDE exclusively and verify the package.json product name.

Changed:

- Display name updated to "Antigravity IDE Editor" and the External Tools entry is now always labelled "Antigravity IDE".
- Insider builds (Antigravity IDE - Insider / antigravity-ide-insiders) are now recognised on Windows, macOS and Linux.
- License changed from MIT to dual-license: inherited upstream code (Unity Technologies, Microsoft) stays MIT; new contributions are PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0. Commercial use requires a separate license — open a GitHub issue. Releases up to v1.0.5 remain available under MIT.
